data.title: US Vice President Vance reiterated that the deadline for sanctions against Iran is 8 p.m. Eastern Time on Tuesday.
data.description: US Vice President Vance reiterated that the deadline for Iran was 8 p.m. Eastern Time on Tuesday, and stated that Kharg Island did not signify a strategic shift. This followed a strike on military targets on Kharg Island earlier Tuesday (local time) by two US officials, who stated that the US struck more than 50 targets on the island ahead of Trump's deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Officials said the strike took place early Tuesday morning Eastern Time and did not target oil infrastructure. Trump had set Tuesday evening (Eastern Time) as the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, otherwise it risked a devastating blow to its energy infrastructure. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Vice President Reaffirms Deadline for Iran Amid Military Strikes
data.description: U.S. Vice President Vance has reiterated the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz by Tuesday at 8 p.m. Eastern Time, stating that the recent actions on Kharg Island do not signify a strategic shift. According to Odaily, two U.S. officials reported that prior to the deadline set by U.S. President Donald Trump, the United States conducted strikes on military targets on Kharg Island earlier on Tuesday. One official noted that over 50 targets were hit on the island. The strikes, carried out early Tuesday Eastern Time, did not target oil infrastructure. President Trump has set Tuesday evening as the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, warning of potential devastating impacts on energy infrastructure if the deadline is not met.

data.title: U.S. Vice President Vance Comments on Iran Conflict Resolution
data.description:
U.S. Vice President Vance has expressed optimism regarding the conclusion of the conflict with Iran, stating that the military objectives have been achieved. According to Odaily, Vance emphasized the importance of Iran's actions moving forward, expressing hope for wise decisions from the Iranian side.

Additionally, The Wall Street Journal has reported that the United States conducted airstrikes on over 50 military targets on Kharg Island.

data.title: ADP Report Shows Private Sector Job Growth in March
data.description: The ADP weekly employment report reveals that private sector employers added an average of 26,000 jobs per week over the four weeks ending March 21, 2026. According to Odaily, this data highlights ongoing employment trends in the private sector.

data.title: Bit Digital disclosed that its ETH holdings in March were 155,444.4 coins, with the staking ratio significantly reduced to 62%.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Ethereum treasury company Bit Digital released its monthly report, disclosing that it held approximately 155,444.4 ETH as of March 31, 2026, with a market value of approximately $327 million based on a price of $2,104. Furthermore, Bit Digital stated that its ETH staking decreased to 96,322 ETH in March, representing approximately 62% of its total holdings, a move aimed at seeking other yield opportunities and capital allocation plans. (PRNewswire)

data.title: Bitcoin Holds Above $67K as Geopolitics Drive Markets, Wintermute Warns of Fragile Crypto Rally
data.description: Key TakeawaysWintermute says markets are fully driven by Iran conflict headlines.Bitcoin rose ~2%, holding above $67,000 support amid extreme fear sentiment (index at 9).U.S. 10-year yields climbed to 4.36%, with zero probability of near-term Fed cuts.ETF inflows ($1.32B in March) and institutional buying support prices, but whale selling is rising.Geopolitical Headlines Dominate Market DirectionGlobal markets remain highly sensitive to developments in the Iran conflict, according to Wintermute.Last week saw sharp swings across assets:Ceasefire signals pushed equities higher and oil lowerRenewed escalation rhetoric sent oil above $111 and pressured risk assetsOngoing uncertainty around the Strait of Hormuz continues to drive volatilityMarkets are increasingly reacting to alternating signals of escalation and de-escalation, creating a headline-driven environment.Bitcoin Shows Resilience but Lacks MomentumBitcoin rose approximately 2% over the week, narrowly holding above the $67,000 support level.Despite the stability, sentiment remains deeply negative, with the Crypto Fear & Greed Index at 9 (extreme fear)—one of the lowest readings since the conflict began.This divergence highlights a market where price is stable, but confidence remains weak.Macro Pressure Builds as Yields RiseThe macro backdrop continues to tighten.U.S. 10-year Treasury yields have climbed to 4.36%, up 40 basis points since the conflict began, while swap markets are pricing no chance of a Fed rate cut at the upcoming April meeting.Upcoming PCE inflation data is expected to be a key catalyst, particularly as rising oil prices risk feeding into broader inflation metrics.Institutional Demand Supports, But Selling Pressure EmergesInstitutional flows remain a key support pillar:Bitcoin ETFs saw $1.32 billion inflows in MarchStrategy added 44,000 BTCMorgan Stanley received approval for a spot ETF with a 14bps feeHowever, signs of weakening demand are emerging:Crypto funds saw $414 million outflows late MarchExchange whale ratio surged from 0.34 to 0.79, indicating increased large-holder sellingOTC flows shifted from net buying to neutral or sellingEthereum Outperforms, Solana Hit by HackEthereum gained 4.2%, supported by its staking yield, which is becoming more attractive in a “higher-for-longer” rate environment.In contrast, Solana fell below $80 following a $285 million exploit on the Drift protocol, marking one of the largest hacks in its ecosystem.Key Iran Deadline Could Drive Next MoveMarkets are now focused on the upcoming deadline tied to the Strait of Hormuz.A proposed 45-day ceasefire framework represents the most concrete de-escalation effort so far. However, Wintermute warns that structural damage to energy infrastructure means supply disruptions may persist even in a ceasefire scenario.If escalation resumes, particularly following threats from Donald Trump, oil prices could spike again, reinforcing inflation pressures and weighing on risk assets.Stable Prices, Fragile StructureBitcoin’s ability to hold above $67,000 reflects strong institutional support, but underlying conditions remain fragile.With rising yields, geopolitical uncertainty, and increasing whale distribution, the market remains highly sensitive to macro catalysts.The next directional move will likely depend on:Iran conflict developmentsOil price trajectoryFed inflation dataSustainability of institutional inflowsFor now, the crypto market remains range-bound, event-driven, and vulnerable to sudden shifts in sentiment.

data.title: ADP Weekly Employment Report: Private Sector Added an Average of 26,000 Jobs Per Week Over the Four Weeks of March.
data.description: ADP's weekly employment report shows that in the four weeks ending March 21, 2026, private sector employers added an average of 26,000 jobs per week. (Jinshi)

data.title: US Vice President Vance: The war with Iran will end soon.
data.description: US Vice President Vance, speaking about Iran, stated that the war would soon end, that Iran's military objectives had been achieved, and that it was now up to Iran to act, hoping it would be wise. Separately, the Wall Street Journal reported that the US conducted airstrikes on more than 50 military targets on Halg Island. (Jinshi)

data.title: Cardano and Draper Dragon Launch $80 Million Orion Fund for Blockchain Projects
data.description:
Cardano has partnered with venture capital firm Draper Dragon to launch the Orion Fund, targeting a total of $80 million for investment in native and integrated Cardano projects. According to Odaily, the fund aims to focus on Real-World Assets (RWA) and institutional-grade DeFi to enhance Cardano's Total Value Locked (TVL) and on-chain activity.

Managed by Draper Dragon, the Cardano Foundation will provide ecological and technical support as the constitutional manager. Additionally, through Arouet Holdings, a portion of the fund's returns will be redirected to the Cardano treasury.

data.title: Oil Prices Rise Amid Tensions Between U.S. and Iran
data.description:
Oil prices have increased following reports of an explosion on Iran's Kharg Island. According to Odaily, U.S. President Donald Trump stated in a press conference on Monday that if Iran fails to meet the demands by 8 p.m. Eastern Time, the U.S. might destroy all bridges to Iran's power plants within four hours. This escalation could potentially lead to a surge in energy prices.

Additionally, The Wall Street Journal reported that negotiators are pessimistic about Iran meeting Trump's demands to allow ships free passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Despite this, oil prices have not fluctuated significantly, possibly indicating market skepticism about Trump's adherence to his deadline. AJ Bell analyst Dan Coatsworth noted that either Washington or Tehran might concede, potentially causing a significant stock market rebound and a drop in energy prices. However, he also mentioned a third possibility: the deadline could be extended, leading to another period of market uncertainty as both sides' next moves are anticipated.

data.title: Alchemix Relaunches Bug Bounty Program with $300,000 Reward
data.description:
Web3 security platform Immunefi has announced on the X platform that the DeFi protocol Alchemix has relaunched its bug bounty program based on its latest v3 contracts. According to ChainCatcher, the program offers a maximum reward of $300,000.

The initiative aims to encourage security researchers to review the new version of the contract code and identify potential vulnerabilities in advance, thereby enhancing the overall security of the protocol. Immunefi stated that this is a critical window for auditing the relevant code and participating in the bounty program.

data.title: Debate Over Tokenized Equities: Citadel Securities and Blockchain Association Clash in SEC Submissions
data.description: Citadel Securities and the Blockchain Association have presented differing views in their submissions to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) regarding the regulation of tokenized equities. According to NS3.AI, the market for tokenized equities was valued at $946 million in March. Citadel Securities has advocated for maintaining tokenized stock trading within the current definitions of intermediaries, emphasizing the importance of existing regulatory frameworks. In contrast, the Blockchain Association has argued that this approach could mistakenly categorize infrastructure providers as intermediaries, potentially stifling innovation in the sector. The debate highlights the ongoing discussion about how best to regulate emerging financial technologies while balancing innovation and investor protection.

data.title: Bitcoin's Negative Correlation with Global Easing Breadth Index Observed
data.description: Bitcoin has recently shown a significant negative correlation with the Global Easing Breadth Index, which monitors the monetary policy direction of 41 central banks. According to NS3.AI, spot Bitcoin ETFs experienced their largest single-day net inflow since late February this week. Analysts suggest that institutions might be taking advantage of the current price range to accumulate Bitcoin.

data.title: WTI Crude Oil Surges Amid Middle East Tensions, Crypto Sentiment Remains Weak
data.description: Wintermute reported that geopolitical developments in the Middle East have significantly influenced global markets this week, leading to an 11% increase in WTI crude oil prices, which rose above $111. According to NS3.AI, this surge followed U.S. President Donald Trump's firm stance on the situation. Meanwhile, the cryptocurrency market continues to exhibit weak sentiment, as indicated by the Fear & Greed Index, which remains at a low level of 9. Additionally, March saw a shift in ETF flows, transitioning from a $1.32 billion net inflow to net outflows by the end of the month.

data.title: Cardano and Draper Dragon jointly established an $80 million Orion fund, focusing on RWA and institutional-grade DeFi.
data.description: According to the Cardano Foundation's official website, Cardano and venture capital firm Draper Dragon announced the launch of the Orion Fund (Draper Dragon Ecosystem Fund), with a target size of $80 million. The fund will invest in Cardano's native and integrated projects, focusing on Real-World Assets (RWA) and institutional-grade DeFi to enhance Cardano's TVL and on-chain activity. The fund is managed by Draper Dragon, with the Cardano Foundation providing ecosystem and technical support as the constitutional manager. A portion of the proceeds will be channeled back to the Cardano treasury through the ownerless entity Arouet Holdings. Draper University will serve as an acceleration partner, providing startup acceleration and talent development. The fund will prioritize projects that leverage the UTXO model to bridge Bitcoin liquidity and Cardano functionality, and will enhance transparency and community oversight through public KPI dashboards and quarterly ecosystem roundtables.

data.title: Iran Considers Options for Reopening Stock Market Amid Tensions
data.description: The U.S. military has targeted military sites on Kharg Island, according to a U.S. official, as Iran evaluates four potential strategies to reopen its stock market. According to NS3.AI, the head of the Iranian Securities and Exchange Organization outlined options ranging from maintaining fund trading under current conditions to halting all trading if hostilities intensify. Meanwhile, Argus Media data indicates that Urals crude at Port of Primorsk reached $116.05 per barrel on April 2, nearly double the $59 average projected in Russia's budget for this year. The Iranian stock market has been suspended since March 1.

data.title: JUST Platform Shows Steady Growth in TRON DeFi Ecosystem
data.description:
The JUST platform, a key component of the TRON DeFi ecosystem, has demonstrated stable performance this week. According to ChainCatcher, the platform's total value locked reached $11.01 billion, accounting for 41.96% of TRON DeFi's overall volume. The supply on the platform stands at $3.59 billion, with borrowing amounts totaling $210.28 million. Additionally, the JST token buyback and burn mechanism is progressing steadily, with over 1.08 billion tokens destroyed, representing 10.96% of the total supply, thereby reinforcing the token's value through deflationary effects.

The robust locked value data and ongoing buyback and burn activities provide strong support for JUST's long-term development. Moving forward, JUST plans to focus on product iteration and ecosystem construction to enhance capital efficiency and user experience, contributing to the healthy growth of the TRON DeFi ecosystem.

data.title: Global payment platform Paysafe launches "Pay with Crypto" encrypted payment solution
data.description: Global payments platform Paysafe announced the launch of its "Pay with Crypto" crypto payment solution, currently primarily targeting the US iGaming and Daily Fantasy sports markets. The solution allows users to top up their accounts using stablecoins and major cryptocurrencies. Data shows that approximately 70.4 million US adults hold cryptocurrency, and 83% of gamers have expressed interest in crypto payments. Paysafe users can complete payments by connecting their crypto wallets or scanning a QR code; the system will automatically convert fiat currency upon transaction confirmation. (Businesswire)

data.title: The Ministry of State Security warns of the risks of "word-based scams": Claims of "getting rich quick by hoarding words" are suspected of being illegal financial activities and could endanger national economic security.
data.description: An article published on the official WeChat account of the Department of Homeland Security points out that "word-based scams" are emerging one after another, especially those involving "getting rich quick by hoarding words" and "profiting from over-the-counter trading," which are suspected of being illegal financial activities. These scams could be exploited by foreign intelligence agencies to endanger national economic security. While embracing and utilizing new technologies and applications, it is crucial to guard against risks and ensure security, including risks of data leaks and hijacking, forgery and tampering, and fraud traps. In the face of the word-based craze, it is essential to view its value rationally while paying attention to information security and privacy, raising awareness of security precautions, and understanding and using words wisely.

data.title: 360 recently discovered three vulnerabilities in OpenClaw: one high-risk and two medium-risk.
data.description: 360's vulnerability discovery AI recently uncovered and reported three high-value vulnerabilities in OpenClaw: one high-risk and two medium-risk. All newly discovered vulnerabilities have since been patched and publicly disclosed by 360. It is understood that these three newly discovered vulnerabilities directly target the core operating mechanism of the AI ​​agent, directly impacting the core security of user devices, data, and accounts. (The Paper)
